title: Title
description: Project update cover slide — May 2026, Traci Nathans-Kelly and Dr Simon Wang, deep mahogany background.
speakerNotes: Welcome Traci and collaborators. This is a snapshot of where we are — everything covered here either already exists or is one step from being done.

title: Where We Are
description: Four completed deliverables: Listening Demo, Content Library, Authors Found, Permission Letter Template.
speakerNotes: These four things are already done — not planned, done. The listening demo is live, all 127 articles are catalogued, and the permission letter is ready to send the moment you're happy with it.

title: Where This Goes
description: Destination slide — lessons published to the IEEE eLearning Library; two-column layout showing the Library context and the article → lesson → library pipeline.
speakerNotes: Before the demo, it's worth anchoring where all of this ends up. The IEEE eLearning Library already hosts hundreds of courses — engineers, faculty, and students worldwide use it for continuing education. ProComm-generated interactive lessons fit naturally into that catalog. This is the distribution channel that makes the whole pipeline worthwhile.

title: Learner Experience
description: The learner experience — AI-narrated audio, comprehension check, xAPI tracking, PDF certificate. Prototype lesson: Listening as Engineering Communication (Leydens & Lucena, 2009).
speakerNotes: Let me show you the demo. The prototype lesson is based on Leydens and Lucena's 2009 paper in IEEE Transactions on Professional Communication — 'Listening as Engineering Communication'. A learner clicks play, listens to the AI-narrated overview, answers a few comprehension questions, and receives a certificate. Every step is recorded in xAPI.

title: Lesson Creation
description: Author workflow — paste article, AI drafts lesson, author edits, admin publishes. Under 20 minutes.
speakerNotes: This is the author side. A volunteer submits a ProComm article, the AI generates a draft lesson automatically, the author tweaks it, and it goes through a simple admin review before publishing. The whole process is under 20 minutes.

title: Content Library
description: 127 ProComm resources classified — 65 Articles, 44 Resource Links, 9 Podcasts. Top authors. Link to Google Sheet.
speakerNotes: We went through every single article on the ProComm resources page — all 127. 65 are full articles suitable for conversion into lessons. And the top six authors account for about half the content.

title: Author Outreach
description: Permission letter plan — Traci as sender, 14 confirmed emails, Google Doc link to letter template.
speakerNotes: The key insight here is that the letters come from you as IEEE ProComm VP Content — not from a researcher at HKBU. That trust signal is huge. We've already confirmed 14 email addresses.

title: Research Study
description: Three research questions — comprehension, engagement, xAPI learning analytics. Link to research plan Google Doc.
speakerNotes: This study design comes directly from Traci's suggestion. Three clean research questions that fit naturally into what the platform already produces — no extra instrumentation needed beyond what xAPI gives us.

title: What to Teach
description: IEEE Learning Network (ILN) existing ProComm courses and the 9 topic categories our platform extends.
speakerNotes: The IEEE Learning Network already has two ProComm courses — so there's an established audience and precedent. Our platform doesn't compete with ILN; it extends it by converting the article library into interactive lessons across all 9 topic categories.

title: Publication Pipeline
description: Three outputs — ProComm 2026 workshop paper (done), TPC Teaching Case Submission (June), companion study (proposed).
speakerNotes: One platform, three publications. The workshop paper is already submitted. The Teaching Case is a specific IEEE TPC submission format that documents a real instructional case — in preparation for June. The empirical study is the one we'd co-author together.

title: Timeline
description: April–Fall 2026 milestones — paper done, outreach in May, Teaching Case in June, ProComm Edmonton July 12–15.
speakerNotes: The camera-ready deadline was April 30 — that's already done. The most pressing next step is the outreach in May, which is why we're meeting now.

title: Next Steps
description: Four asks from Traci — send permissions (including LinkedIn outreach to Bob Lyons and Samartha Vashishtha), research proposal, Alan Chong intro, eLearning conversion plan.
speakerNotes: Four concrete asks. The most immediate is the permission letters — review and send the 14 emails we've prepared, and we'll handle the LinkedIn messages to Bob Lyons and Samartha Vashishtha at the same time. The bigger open question I'd love your thinking on today is how you see the eLearning Library conversion working — who decides, at what cadence, and whether there's an IEEE editorial process we should be designing around.
